MSK: Chronic arthritic changes in multiple joints Back Exam: Inspection: no muscle atrophy, reversal of normal lordosis and exaggeration of thoracic kyphosis Palpation: TTP bilateral facet joints and paraspinal musculature ROM: limited especially lateral rotation, pain on flexion and lateral rotation Facet loading: RIGHT- positive/ LEFT- positive. Assessment: Assessment: 1. Lumbar radiculopathy - M54.16 (Primary) 2. Spondylosis of lumbosacral joint without myelopathy - M47.817 3. Neuropathy - G62.9 4. Long term (current) use of opiate analgesic - Z79.891 History- He incurred pain on August 15, 2015, he slid and fell on the floor when he was mopping. He began to feel numbness and weakness to his legs. He saw pain management and had two epidural injections. This did not help. He did physical therapy in 2015 and repeated again this year, the month prior to this initial appointment. He had discectomy on 8/17/22 at the back institute in Los Angeles. He was searching online and found this surgeon and had great testimonies. Per Dr. Jagadesh, no surgery indicated. He has more pain now. He cannot stand for longer than 10 minutes whereas before he could for 30 minutes. He is not able to work. I reviewed CT of the lumbar spine performed on August 16, 2022 which revealed mild posterior disc bulge at multiple levels. There is mild facet arthropathy from L1-L5. There is moderate canal stenosis at L5-S1 with mild to moderate bilateral neural foraminal narrowing. At L4-5 there is severe central canal stenosis and moderate bilateral neural foraminal narrowing. L3-4 there is severe central canal stenosis as well as at L2-3. Reviewed an x-ray of the lumbar spine which revealed no evidence of fracture. EMG that was performed in 9/30/2022 was normal per patient. It was recommended that he have a neurological consultation since he has not had relief from spinal injections and was not a surgical candidate. There may be a neurological condition that may be causing his pain. Pt did have neurology follow up and had a new LE EMG. Per report he is positive for L L3-4 radiculopathy. This was not identified on his last EMG. Today: patient is a pleasant 43-year-old male who is presenting for lumbar radiculopathy down the back of both legs. He reports that he did have a left L4 and L5 laminectomy in Africa about a year ago. Reports that he continues to have pain down both legs. Discussed lumbar MRI in detail today with patient. He would like to proceed with an epidural. He reports no surgeries at L5 and S1. Discussed ILESI L5/S1 in detail with patient. He would like to proceed. Denies any use of blood thinners. Reports that he would like to trial physical therapy. He would also like to hold off any surgical evaluations. Denies neurological deficits, red flag symptoms, or severe pain. ER precautions given. He would like a Toradol injection today due to pain. He reports that pain is worsened with standing for long period of time, walking for long period of time, sitting to standing positions as well. No pain in the buttocks or SI joint. Reports that he cannot do much for over 30 minutes before having pain. Discussed conservative measures and activity modification in detail as well. He would like to have increase in gabapentin. he would like to trial 400 mg 3 times daily first before increasing any further. Declines LSO today.
